div1.entryFree: Scamble, to scramble, to struggle: --ing, outfacing, fashion-monging boys, Ado V, 1, 94. England now is left to tug and s. and to part by the teeth the unowed interest of proud-swelling state, John IV, 3, 146. the --ing and unquiet time, H5 I, 1, 4. I get thee with --ing, and thou must therefore needs prove a good soldier-breeder, V, 2, 218.
